title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, of Marston Mills, was arrested in Miami Beach for allegedly stabbing his friend during a bachelor party.
Witnesses reported a fight over women led to a stabbing incident involving Dickerson and victim Brian Gordon, who were sharing accommodations.
Dickerson faces an aggravated battery charge; both men were hospitalized, with Dickerson held without bond in Florida.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four games this season.
St. Louis scored three unanswered goals, overcoming an early lead by New England's Ilay Feingold.
Forward Leo Campana exited due to an injury unrelated to his previous one, awaiting further evaluation.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Authorities in Massachusetts and New Hampshire issued warnings about fake court notices from scammers.
Fake notices contain errors, such as misspelled 'Massachusetts,' and request fines via a QR code.
Residents advised to ignore the scam notices and verify any claims using official court contact information.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, was shot by a colleague in her home during an armed confrontation.
Fitzsimmons faces assault charges and has pleaded not guilty; her trial begins March 23 in Essex Superior Court.
Fitzsimmons waived her right to a jury trial; Judge Jeffrey Karp will preside over the bench trial.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, 44, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Originally from Cali, Colombia, Mazariegos views running as a process of personal growth and self-understanding.
She is inspired by those rebuilding their lives through Spaulding, promoting perseverance and shared hope.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues on March 20, 2026, showcasing their latest album and classic hits.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist energized the audience with dynamic showmanship and engaging persona.
Opening band The Chats delivered an energetic, garage rock performance, complementing The Hives' headlining set.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
After a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh underwent chemotherapy and surgeries before resuming her running passion.
Inspired by her late friend Susan Hurley, McHugh aims to prove cancer does not define her as she runs again.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senator Ed Markey and others express privacy concerns over Meta's potential facial recognition in smart glasses.
The senators' letter warns Meta's technology could surveil individuals at protests, affecting privacy and free expression.
Meta has until April 6 to respond about data handling and opt-out options for its facial recognition plans.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed the decision on a $2.65 million bond for new firefighter gear free of PFAS.
Firefighters are pushing for the approval, citing health risks from PFAS chemicals in current gear.
Some councilors want more time to evaluate the gear proposal, considering cost and durability concerns.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro officials propose transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve and wildlife rehabilitation center due to high costs.
The zoo faces $57 million in needed repairs and annual operating costs of $900,000, amid declining attendance.
A transition team will be formed to manage the proposed changes, with the current zoo operations continuing through September 7.
